prompt:
Our monorepo build tool describes internal components with little manifest files and we've started shipping import cycles that only blow up at link time. I want a proper analyzer library: module go-modcycles, package modcycles, stdlib only. Please structure it like a real project — manifest parsing, the graph, cycle enumeration, and topological ordering belong in separate files, not one giant modcycles.go.

The manifest format: every file ending in .mod, anywhere under a root directory (other files ignored). Lines are trimmed; blank lines and lines starting with # are comments. The first real line must be `module <name>`, exactly once per file; every following line is `import <name>`. Tokens are whitespace-separated (extra spaces fine). Names match [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ only. Parse errors, a missing/duplicate module line, junk directives, and importing the same name twice in one file are all Load errors that NAME THE FILE in the message; the same module declared in two files is an error naming the module. Modules may import modules nobody declares — that is NOT a load error, people delete manifests all the time.

API: Load(root string) (*Graph, error), then on the graph: Modules() []string sorted; Imports(name) ([]string, bool) in declaration order; Missing() []string — sorted, deduplicated names that are imported but declared nowhere.

Cycles() [][]string is the heart of it: EVERY elementary cycle in the import graph, self-imports included as a one-element cycle. Each cycle is written as the full path — consecutive elements follow import edges and the last element imports the first — rotated to start at its alphabetically smallest member, and the outer list sorted lexicographically element-by-element. Same tree in, byte-identical report out, every single run; the tool diffs these reports in CI.

Order() ([]string, error): dependency-first topological order over declared modules (imports of undeclared names just don't count), and when several modules are simultaneously ready, alphabetical order picks next — again, fully deterministic. If the graph has any cycle, Order returns an error whose text contains "cycle".

Acceptance is load_test.go + cycles_test.go, run with go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — those files are frozen. Keep it stdlib.
